title: Wavelength drift suppression for burst-mode tunable eml laser
abstract: A method (700) of biasing a tunable laser (310) during burst-on and burst-off states includes receiving a burst mode signal (514) indicative of the burst-on state or the burst-off state, and when the burst mode signal is indicative of the burst-on state: delivering a first bias current (I GAIN ) to an anode of a gain-section diode (590a) disposed on a shared substrate of the tunable laser; and delivering a second bias current (I PH ) to an anode of a phase-section diode (590b) disposed on the shared substrate. The second bias current is less than the first bias current. When the burst mode signal transitions to be indicative of the burst-off state, the method also includes: delivering the first bias current to the anode of the gain-section diode; and delivering the second bias current to the anode of the phase-section diode, wherein the first bias current is less than the second bias current.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method (700) comprising: receiving, at a laser driving circuit (500), a burst mode signal (514) indicative of a burst-on state or a burst-off state; when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-on state: delivering, by the laser driving circuit (500), a first bias current (IGAIN) to an anode of a gain-section diode (590a) (590a) disposed on a shared substrate of a tunable laser (310); and delivering, by the laser driving circuit (500), a second bias current (IPH) to an anode of a phase-section diode (590b) disposed on the shared substrate of the tunable laser (310), wherein the second bias current (IPH) is less than the first bias current (IGAIN); and when the burst mode signal (514) transitions to be indicative of the burst-off state from the burst-on state: delivering, by the laser driving circuit (500), the first bias current (IGAIN) to the anode of the gain-section diode (590a); and delivering, by the laser driving circuit (500), the second bias current (IPH) to the anode of the phase-section diode (590b), wherein the first bias current (IGAIN) is less than the second bias current (IPH). 2. The method (700) of claim 1, wherein: the first bias current (IGAIN) is equal to a tail current (IDC) minus the second bias current (IPH); and the second bias current (IPH) is equal to the tail current (IDC) minus the first bias current (IGAIN). 3. The method (700) of claim 1 or 2, further comprising receiving, at the laser driving circuit (500), a tail current adjustment from a burst control stage (506) of the laser driving circuit (500), the tail current adjustment configured to adjust a tail current (IDC). 4. The method (700) of claim 3, wherein the tail current adjustment is connected to a voltage source (540). 5. The method (700) of claim 3 or 4, wherein the tail current adjustment is connected to a power management integrated circuit (592). 6. The method (700) of any of claims 3-5, wherein the burst control stage (506) comprises a differential pair of first and second MOSFETs (520), each MOSFET (520) connected to a burst mode signal source (512), the first MOSFET (520) connected to the anode of the gain-section diode (590a), the second MOSFET (520) connected to the anode of the phase-section diode (590b). 7. The method (700) of claim 6, wherein the first MOSFET (520) is turned off and the second MOSFET (520) is turned on when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-off state. 8. The method (700) of claim 6 or 7, wherein the first MOSFET (520) is turned on and the second MOSFET (520) is turned off when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-on state. 9. The method (700) of any of claims 1-8, further comprising modulating, by the laser driving circuit (500), the laser (310) by a capacitively coupled modulation stage (504) of the laser driving circuit (500) to an anode of an Electro- Absorption-section diode (590c), resulting in an alternating current (AC) modulation current. 10. The method (700) of claim 9, wherein the capacitively coupled modulation stage (504) comprises a differential pair of first and second MOSFETs (520), the first MOSFET (520) connected to a positive data signal source (510) and to a first resistor (530), the first resistor (530) connected to a voltage source (540), the second MOSFET (520) connected to a negative data signal source (510), a second resistor (530), and a 

 capacitor (580), the second resistor (530) connected to the voltage source (540), and the capacitor (580) connected to the anode of the Electro- Absorption-section diode (590c). 11. The method (700) of claim 10, wherein the capacitor (580) is connected to an inductor (570) connected to a variable voltage source (552). 12. The method (700) of any of claims 1-11, wherein the tunable laser (310) comprises an Electro-absorption Modulated tunable laser (310). 13. A laser driving circuit (500) comprising: a tail current source (550b) configured to: deliver a first bias current (IGAIN) to an anode of a gain-section diode (590a) disposed on a shared substrate of a tunable laser (310); and deliver a second bias current (IPH) to an anode of a phase-section diode (590b) disposed on the shared substrate of the tunable laser (310); a burst control stage (506) configured to receive a burst mode signal (514) indicative of a burst-on state or a burst-off state, the burst control stage (506) configured to: when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-off state, deliver the first bias current (IGAIN) to the anode of the gain-section diode (590a) and deliver the second bias current (IPH) to the anode of the phase-section diode (590b), wherein the first bias current (IGAIN) is less than the second bias current (IPH); and when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-on state, deliver the first bias current (IGAIN) to the anode of the gain-section diode (590a) and deliver the second bias current (IPH) to the anode of the phase-section diode (590b), wherein the first bias current (IGAIN) is greater than the second bias current (IPH). 14. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 13, wherein: the first bias current (IGAIN) is equal to a tail current (IDC) minus the second bias current (IPH); and 

 the second bias current (IPH) is equal to the tail current (IDC) minus the first bias current (IGAIN). 15. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 13 or 14, further comprising a tail current adjustment configured to adjust a tail current (IDC). 16. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 15, wherein the tail current adjustment is connected to a voltage source (540). 17. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 15 or 16, wherein the tail current adjustment is connected to a power management integrated circuit (592). 18. The laser driving circuit (500) of any of claims 15-17, further comprising a differential pair of first and second MOSFETs (520), each MOSFET (520) connected to a burst mode signal source (512), the first MOSFET (520) connected to the anode of the gain-section diode (590a), the second MOSFET (520) connected to the anode of the phase-section diode (590b). 19. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 18, wherein the first MOSFET (520) is turned off and the second MOSFET (520) is turned on when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-off state. 20. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 18 or 19, wherein the first MOSFET (520) is turned on and the second MOSFET (520) is turned off when the burst mode signal (514) is indicative of the burst-on state. 21. The laser driving circuit (500) of any of claims 13-20, further comprising a modulation stage (504) capacitively coupled to an anode of an Electro- Absorption- section diode (590c), resulting in an alternating current (AC) modulation current. 22. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 21, wherein the modulation stage (504) comprises a differential pair of first and second MOSFETs (520), the first MOSFET (520) connected to a positive data signal source (510) and to a first resistor (530), the first resistor (530) connected to a voltage source (540), the second MOSFET (520) connected to a negative data signal source (510), a second resistor (530), and a capacitor (580), the second resistor (530) connected to the voltage source (540), and the capacitor (580) connected to the anode of the Electro- Absorption-section diode (590c). 23. The laser driving circuit (500) of claim 22, wherein the capacitor (580) is connected to an inductor (570) connected to a variable voltage source (552). 24. The laser driving circuit (500) of any of claims 13-23, wherein the tunable laser (310) comprises an Electro-absorption Modulated tunable laser (310).
